free_text: Cross sections of medium mass nuclide activation
*By compiler*. Monitor cross section at 30 MeV as
recommended by Sandor in D4110003 is approx. 190 mb,
which is significantly smaller than monitor cross-
section adopted by Levkovski and equal 250 mb.
data given. 6-th edition of Lederer's Handbook
Rotating target contained 15 samples and three
monitoring Mo-foils. Each sample had been covered
by layers of aluminum. This construction permitted
to bombard every target by several energies. In most
cases samples (in form of oxide in aluminum foil) were
isotopically enriched to 95-98%; target area of 1 cm2;
Energy uncertainty for alpha-particle beam low than
30 MeV is 1-2 MeV, approximately. If beam energy is
low than 15 MeV, then energy uncertainty is grown up
The uncertainty in the cross section is 10%.
The uncertainty in the monitor reaction
for Tc-96 indicated by author in text at 30 MeV.
BIB section updated. Monitor value for
Mo-0(p,x)Tc-96 was added, according to N.Otsuka remarks
SD: MONITOR reactions moved to relevant
Subents according to alpha- and proton-induced reaction
codes. Part of CRITIQUE moved to REL-REF according to
information from the book on p.3. REACTION code
correction in several Subents (see HISTORY in Subents).
